title: ranunculus aquatilis f. rigidus
intro: Ranunculus aquatilis f. rigidus, also known as the white water crowfoot, is a subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the buttercup family and is native to North America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The plant is rich in bioactive compounds that have been shown to have various health benefits, including antioxidant, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory activities, making it a potential natural remedy for various health conditions. In herbal preparations, Ranunculus aquatilis f. rigidus is often used in teas, infusions, and topical applications, and can be dried or made into capsules for internal use.
